The assignment

Questions & Sample Answers

Question 1

[1] "What illness was Greta Thunberg diagnosed with when she was eleven years old?"
 [1] She was diagnosed with asperger’s syndrome.                                                    
 [2] She was diagnosed with Asperger syndrome,OCD and selective mutism.                             
 [3] She was diagnosed with Asperger’s syndrome when she was eleven years old.                      
 [4]                                                                                                
 [5] She has Asperger syndrome.                                                                     
 [6] It is depression and the asperger syndrome.                                                    
 [7]                                                                                                
 [8] Asperger’s syndrome.                                                                           
 [9] She was diagnosed with Asperger's syndrome, obsessive-compulsive disorder and selective mutism.
[10]                                                                                                
8 Levels:  ... She was diagnosed with asperger’s syndrome.

Question 2

[1] "According to the speaker, how many species become extinct every day?"
 [1] 200                                               
 [2]                                                   
 [3] Up to 200 species become extinct every day.       
 [4] 200 species.                                      
 [5] 200 species                                       
 [6] About 200 species.                                
 [7] About 200 species per day.                        
 [8] With up to 200 species becoming extinct every day.
 [9] About 200 species become extinct every day.       
[10]                                                   
9 Levels:  200 200 species ... With up to 200 species becoming extinct every day.

Question 3

[1] "What is the one thing that we need now, according to the speaker?"
 [1] We need to treat climate change as a crisis.                                     
 [2] We need to take action now.                                                      
 [3] We need to wake up and change.                                                   
 [4] The one thing we need now is action, not discussion.                             
 [5] One thing we need more than hope we need action Look for walk and look for action
 [6] The one thing that we need more than hope is action.                             
 [7]                                                                                  
 [8] Our actual commitments to the Paris Agreement.                                   
 [9] We need to think about the situation and care about the Earth.                   
[10]                                                                                  
9 Levels:  ...

Question 4:

[1] "What are there no rules for?"
 [1] There are no rules to keep oil in the ground.    
 [2] There are no rules for keeping oil in the ground.
 [3]                                                  
 [4]                                                  
 [5]                                                  
 [6] Saving the world.                                
 [7]                                                  
 [8]                                                  
 [9]                                                  
[10] For keeping oil in the ground.                   
5 Levels:  For keeping oil in the ground. ... There are no rules to keep oil in the ground.

Question 5:

[1] "What do you want to do now having watched Thunberg`s speech?"
 [1] I want to start small things by changing my lifestyle as much as possible to protect our future and environment.                                             
 [2] I want to take care for environment and check my own habits whether my actions are good or bad for the environment. For example,                             
 [3] I want to take action in my daily life,such as reducing waste and saving energy.                                                                             
 [4]                                                                                                                                                              
 [5] I want to take small actions to protect the environment instead of just talking about hope.                                                                  
 [6] I want to take action to protect the environment. For example, I want to save energy and reduce waste in my daily life.                                      
 [7] I want to reduce waste and save energy.                                                                                                                      
 [8] Change my act.                                                                                                                                               
 [9] I cannot eliminate all emissions from now on, but I want to try to care about climate change from my side. Moreover, I want to take part in events like that.
[10] Look for action.                                                                                                                                             
10 Levels:  ... Look for action.


Summaries

five sample summaries

[1] She thinks that climate change and global warming is led by humans and has doubts why people spend their time not having changed before, limited, and illegal. As a result ,a lot of species around the world are being extinct, and its speed is much faster. People do not take action because they do not visually realize the disposal of human lives especially collapsing construction and so on. She believes that if she had a family or some communities in the future, she would be asked by her children and grandchildren that people in 2018 could take actions to climate change and global warming, but why did they not take actions. Action for climate change and global warming affect the nature and environment of the next generation. For solving climate change and global warming, and for having hopes in the future, people must need “Action” rather than saying about environmental changes.                                                                                                                           
[2]                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
[3] The speaker talks about the problem of climate change. She started to learn about global warming when she was eight years old/She felt very strange because people were not acting like they were in a world war. When she was eleven, she became very sad and she stopped eating because she was worried about the environment and she lost too much weight. She was diagnosed with Asperger syndrome,OCD and selective mutism, and they make her mind as black or white. She cannot have a choice of the gray zone. She speaks that rich countries must reduce emissions very fast so that poor countries can improve their lives. She also says that more than 200 species become extinct every single day, even though politicians and media do not mention it. She started a strike in the school because she thought a strike was more important than studying in the class. Finally, she says that we do not need just hope. We need action first. Then hope will follow. She believes we must change the rules of society to save the world.
[4] Greta first heard of environmental problems when she was eight years old. She thought it strange that humans are only one kind of animal, but they have the power to change the Earth's climate. Even in a world where recycling is claimed, fuel consumption is not illegal, and it is not a big problem. This reality shocked her and made her sick. Through this speech, she is making a horn in the world. Many media outlets have spoken out about environmental issues, but on the other hand, they have not mentioned specific figures on environmental issues or measures that we must take. She judges things by black or white, so she can't forgive the trend of the world. One of the reasons why we are reluctant to act is that many people don't know how continuing their lives will affect the future. From this moment on, she says it's important for us to think about the future and act. Action is more important than hope.                                                                                                  
[5]                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
4 Levels:  ...


Plagiarism Check

What was the longest string of words shared among the students’ summaries and transcript?
                                                           collocation count
1 she was diagnosed with asperger’s syndrome when she was eleven years     2
2          it made me realize that climate change is a serious problem     2
Comment: Well remembered. Common English.

word count for summary


Opinions

five sample opinions

[1]                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
[2]                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
[3] I agree with her statement. We need to take action right now against climate change. If it is true what she said that the reason why we would not take any action is because we do not have a clue about the consequences of our life, the thing we have to do at first is to know accurately what is going on on the earth on my own.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
[4] When I was young, I did not think about the problems of the world, so I had not ever thought about it. She was said should go to the school by everyone ,but I think that this is great she has questions to the problem to the world, and speak up to the world.Otherwise, she should study if she wants to be a scientist, because if she study science she can learn and get information about the global warming and climate changes.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
[5] As she says, I think people still have time to deal with environmental problems. It will be necessary to live thinking about the impact of our current lives on the future. But if you ask yourself what you can do for environmental problems in your life, you won't get an answer right away. I can only think of ideas that are common and concrete, such as reducing or recycling garbage. She said that action is more important than finding hope, but I felt that I didn't know what action to take. But I thought thinking about what I could do was the first thing I could do. What I can do is very small, but I hope I can make some difference by continuing to think about environmental issues and future life every day.
4 Levels:  ...
